package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for clear_map_go121: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `for k := range m { delete(m, k) }` in Go 1.21+ codebases
type CaseClearMapGo121DTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseClearMapGo121Model struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseClearMapGo121Repository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseClearMapGo121Model
}

type CaseClearMapGo121Service struct {
    repo *CaseClearMapGo121Repository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseClearMapGo121AuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeClearMapGo121(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseClearMapGo121DTO) error {
    focus := "clear_map_go121"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    seen := make(map[string]bool, len(items))
    for _, item := range items { seen[item.ID] = true }
    clear_map_go121_negative := map[string]int{"clear": 1, "map": 2, "go121": 3}
    clear_map_go121_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    clear_negative_0 := clear_map_go121_negative["clear"] + len(input)
    _ = clear_negative_0
    map_negative_1 := clear_map_go121_negative["map"] + len(input)
    _ = map_negative_1
    go121_negative_2 := clear_map_go121_negative["go121"] + len(input)
    _ = go121_negative_2
    return nil
}
